discoveries.name: Saguaro Cactus
discoveries.description: A tall branching cactus that can store large amounts of water.
discoveries.educational_fact: Saguaros grow slowly and provide nesting spaces, flowers, and fruit for desert animals.
discoveries.location_hint: Look for tall silhouettes on open, sunny desert slopes.

discoveries.name: Prickly Pear Cactus
discoveries.description: A flat-padded cactus with fruits and protective spines.
discoveries.educational_fact: The pads store water and the fruit feeds animals and people when harvested carefully.
discoveries.location_hint: Search low sunny areas near rocks and trail edges.

discoveries.name: Creosote Bush
discoveries.description: A hardy shrub with small waxy leaves and a strong desert-rain smell.
discoveries.educational_fact: Waxy leaves reduce water loss, helping creosote survive long dry periods.
discoveries.location_hint: Look across dry flats where shrubs are widely spaced.

discoveries.name: Mesquite Tree
discoveries.description: A drought-tolerant tree with pods, shade, and deep roots.
discoveries.educational_fact: Deep roots help mesquite reach underground water and create habitat for other species.
discoveries.location_hint: Check washes, yards, and slightly lower areas where water collects.

discoveries.name: Palo Verde Tree
discoveries.description: A green-barked tree that can photosynthesize through its trunk and branches.
discoveries.educational_fact: Green bark lets the tree keep making food even when it drops leaves to save water.
discoveries.location_hint: Look for bright green trunks near washes and desert roadsides.

discoveries.name: Agave
discoveries.description: A rosette plant with thick pointed leaves and strong fibers.
discoveries.educational_fact: Agave fibers can be used for cordage, showing how plant structure can inspire materials.
discoveries.location_hint: Search rocky sunny patches and dry slopes.

discoveries.name: Yucca
discoveries.description: A spiky desert plant with tough leaves and useful fibers.
discoveries.educational_fact: Yucca fibers and natural compounds can be used in tools, baskets, and soap-like mixtures.
discoveries.location_hint: Look for clusters of sword-like leaves in open desert.

discoveries.name: Ocotillo
discoveries.description: A tall plant with many thin canes that leaf out after rain.
discoveries.educational_fact: Ocotillo responds quickly to rain by growing leaves when water is available.
discoveries.location_hint: Look for fountain-shaped canes on rocky slopes.

discoveries.name: Barrel Cactus
discoveries.description: A round ribbed cactus with strong spines and water-storing tissue.
discoveries.educational_fact: Its ribs let the cactus expand after rain and shrink during drought.
discoveries.location_hint: Search sunny rocky places and dry hillsides.

discoveries.name: Jojoba
discoveries.description: A shrub with seeds that contain a stable liquid wax.
discoveries.educational_fact: Jojoba wax is useful because it resists drying and can protect surfaces.
discoveries.location_hint: Look for rounded shrubs in dry scrub habitat.

discoveries.name: Coyote
discoveries.description: A clever desert predator and scavenger.
discoveries.educational_fact: Coyotes help balance food webs by eating small mammals, fruit, insects, and carrion.
discoveries.location_hint: Look for tracks near washes, edges, and early morning paths.

discoveries.name: Javelina
discoveries.description: A social desert mammal often seen in small groups.
discoveries.educational_fact: Javelinas eat cactus pads, roots, and fruits, linking plants to larger desert animals.
discoveries.location_hint: Search near prickly pear, mesquite, and shaded washes.

discoveries.name: Roadrunner
discoveries.description: A fast-running bird that hunts insects, lizards, and small animals.
discoveries.educational_fact: Roadrunners conserve energy by moving on foot and using shade in hot conditions.
discoveries.location_hint: Watch open ground near brushy cover.

discoveries.name: Gila Monster
discoveries.description: A slow-moving venomous lizard with bright patterned scales.
discoveries.educational_fact: Gila monsters spend much time underground and store energy in their tails.
discoveries.location_hint: Look for signs near rocky shelters; observe from a safe distance.

discoveries.name: Desert Tortoise
discoveries.description: A burrowing reptile adapted to dry desert life.
discoveries.educational_fact: Burrows help tortoises escape heat and create shelter used by other animals too.
discoveries.location_hint: Look for burrow entrances near firm sandy soil and low shrubs.
